title: invitations — field-level data classification
description: Every column on both schemas classified by sensitivity tier. Drives DSAR export scope, right-to-erasure behaviour, encryption-at-rest policy. Field paths: `Model.field` — dotted paths reference JSONB inner properties.

tiers.public: Non-identifying, safe to expose in logs / URLs.
tiers.internal: Platform-internal state. Never surfaced to end users. Not personally identifiable.
tiers.confidential: PII that is exportable on DSAR and erasable on request. Encrypted in transit + AES-256 at rest.
tiers.restricted: Credential-adjacent secrets. Encrypted with KMS key destruction on erasure (crypto-shredding). Access logged.
tiers.regulated_minor: PII of a subject under the age of consent — triggers additional COPPA + GDPR Art. 8 controls.

regulated_minor: Not applicable to Invitation directly. When Invitation.role_key implies a guardian-of-minor relationship (owned by future safeguarding module), the target row (Athlete) carries the minor's PII and gets the regulated_minor tier — invitations themselves stay `confidential`.

erasureBehaviour.confidential: purge-with-audit-record — Invitation.email → `erased-<hash>@invalid.local`, message → NULL, InvitationEvent ip_address / user_agent / city → NULL. Country_code retained (aggregate deliverability, no individual identification).
erasureBehaviour.restricted: crypto-shred — token_hash cannot be reversed (SHA-256), and the raw token was never persisted; effectively a no-op. State flipped to `revoked` if not terminal.
